method_atom: A finite-size scaling analysis derived from renormalization-group methods that predicts how the zeros of the partition function and thermodynamic functions of a many-component interacting system behave near a critical point when the system sits at the marginal (upper critical) dimension where fluctuations are only marginally relevant. The leading behaviour is mean-field-like but carries multiplicative logarithmic corrections tied to the vanishing of the effective coupling in the scaling limit, and these corrections split into a component-count-independent part for odd quantities and a component-count-dependent part for even ones, giving a non-perturbative diagnostic of that coupling's triviality.
object_atom: The O(N) phi^4 scalar field theory in four spacetime dimensions (with the N=1 Ising model as the concrete numerical test case), for which the finite-size scaling of its partition-function zeros probes the triviality of phi^4_4 for all N.
brief_reason: The renormalization-group finite-size scaling of partition-function zeros with mean-field-plus-logarithmic corrections at the upper critical dimension is the transferable machinery; the four-dimensional O(N) phi^4 / Ising field theory is the physics-specific object it is applied to.
